title: Microsegmentation
description: Dividing a network into small isolated segments with granular access controls to limit lateral movement after a breach.
fullExplanation: Traditional network segmentation uses VLANs and firewalls to create large zones. Microsegmentation goes further — applying fine-grained policies at the workload level (individual VMs, containers, or processes). Even within the same VLAN, workloads can only communicate if explicitly permitted. This limits blast radius: if an attacker compromises one workload, they cannot move laterally to others. Implemented via software-defined networking (SDN), hypervisor-based firewalls, or service mesh.

approaches.description: VLANs, ACLs, and firewall rules at network level. Traditional but coarse-grained.
Firewall rules enforced at vSwitch level — per-VM policies. VMware NSX, AWS Security Groups.
Agent on each workload enforces policy. Works across any network. Illumio, Guardicore.
mTLS between microservices — Istio, Linkerd. Identity-based, not IP-based.

keyFacts: East-west traffic: lateral movement between workloads — microsegmentation controls this
North-south traffic: in/out of data center — traditional firewalls handle this
Zero Trust principle: default deny between all workloads, allow only what's needed
VMware NSX: distributed firewall in hypervisor — policy follows VM regardless of location
AWS Security Groups: stateful per-instance firewall — microsegmentation in cloud
Kubernetes Network Policies: restrict pod-to-pod communication
Blast radius reduction: compromised workload cannot reach other workloads
